entry.content:
Establish the importance of the topic as a problem to be addressed

X is a major problem in …
Of particular concern is …
One of the main obstacles …

X impacts negatively upon a range of …
It is now well established that X can impair …
X is a growing public health concern worldwide.
The main challenge faced by many researchers is the …
X is one of the most frequently stated problems with …
Lack of X has existed as a health problem for many years.
Xs are one of the most rapidly declining groups of insects in …
X is the leading cause of death in western-industrialised countries.
There is increasing concern that some Xs are being disadvantaged …
There is an urgent need to address the safety problems caused by …

Synthesising material: bringing sources together

Smith argues that/sees X as …. Likewise/Similarly/Whilst/Simultaneous, Wang holds the view/asserts that …

Some authors have mainly been interested in questions concerning X and Y , Jones ….

Others have highlighted the relevance of …. But Smith (2008) is much more concerned with …

Smith (2013) found that X accounted for 30% of Y.

Giving reasons why a method was adopted or rejected

adopted

X was selected for its reliability and validity.
This method is particularly useful in studying …
The design of the questionnaires was based on …
The X approach has a number of attractive features: …
The study uses qualitative analysis in order to gain insights into …
One advantage of the X analysis is that it avoids the problem of …

rejected

A major problem with the experimental method is that …
The main disadvantage of the experimental method is that …
However, there are certain drawbacks associated with the use of …
There are certain problems with the use of focus groups. One of these is that there is less …

Sequence words

Prior to
After
On
Once
Following
When
In the follow-up phase of the study,
The final stage of the study

Indicating methodolgical problems or limitations

In particular, the analysis of X was problematic.
In observational studies, there is a potential for bias from …
The small size of the dataset meant that it was not possible to …
Further data collection is required to determine exactly how X affects Y.
Another major source of uncertainty is in the method used to calculate X.
In this investigation there are several sources for error. The main error is …
It was not possible to investigate the significant relationships of X and Y further because…
The responses relating to X were subjective and were therefore susceptible to recall bias.

you may need this which is from the TPO51Passage1

  • A.

    Two simultaneous developments are described, as well as the reasons why neither one would have occurred without the other.

  • B.

    A hypothesis is presented, and then points in favor of that hypothesis as well as points against it are discussed.

  • C.

    A major event is described, and then the most obvious effects of that event are presented.

  • D.

    A decision is described, and then one likely motivation for that decision is presented.

go beyond mere success 超越单纯的成功

drive a wedge betwenn A and B

break the seal of sth.

sb. are placed ideally to do sth.
sb.处于做sth.的理想位置

at the junction of
It was at the junction of a major trade route with the Nile valley.

sth-directed 以sth为导向的
state-directed economy 以国家为导向的经济

prehistorical 史前的
predynastic 王朝统治以前的

drill cores 岩心

scotoma 盲点

blusterous beast 洪水猛兽

preoccupation 全神贯注的

affirm v.肯定，断言，证实

comtemplate v.沉思，打算，注视

feel dubious about sth. 对sth.表示怀疑

spearhead 引领

rights-of-way 通行权

municipal/local governments

in times of trouble 在困难的时期

cut sb. some slack 放sb.一马

expedient 权宜的，权宜之计

eulogy 悼词

knit and sew / weave 编织

dazzling color 令人眼花缭乱的颜色

persuade 劝说 dissuade 劝阻

tedium 单调的乏味的

to the best of my ability 竭尽我之所能

send my deepest condolences to sb. 向sb致以最深的哀悼

Looking up, I see the immensity of the cosmos;bowing my head, I look at the multitude of the world. The gaze flies, the heart expands, the joy of the senses can reach its peak, and indeed, this is true happiness

the sound of thunder outside piercing in my ears

hypocrite 伪君子

blossom、bloom 开花

throbbing heart 跳动的心

dine v进餐

crusader 十字军

slumber x休眠

karma 宿命，因果报应

divine 神圣的；非凡的；天赐的；极好的

undaunted 顽强不屈的

undaunted 沙漏

strife 争吵
strive 奋斗

foe 仇敌

vow 誓约

A is inseparable from B. A与B形影不离

riveted to my seat 钉在座位上

aired live on TV 通过电视直播

can be put into a textbook 可以写进教科书

engulf v.吞没

deceive v.欺骗

devoid 缺乏的

miracle 奇迹

Under the current educational system, the extracurricular activities designed for students are playing an increasingly important role. Therefore, among parents and teachers, there arises a controversy pertaining whether teenage students should participate in various activities or focus on one activity. Towards such a long-running tug-of-war, I strongly recommend teenagers to engage in more kinds of activities, for the following reasons.

Initially, getting involved in more activities can be conducive to the well rounded development of students. It is self-evident that different activities can equip participants with various skills and abilities. To illustrate, students attending musical classes can learn how to appreciate the classical works of famous musicians and thus improve their aesthetic capability. Similarly, participating in team sports will help students cultivate a sense of cooperation as well as enhance their leadership. After having obtained the above skills, students definitely can develop in an all-round way, with the result of becoming more competitive than others. In contrast, specializing in only one kind of activity is a totally different picture. In other words, students just foster one particular skill,which is a potential obstacle to their future development. The underlying reason is that the more and more complicated modern life necessitates the acquisition of multiple skills.

Furthermore, taking part in many after-class activities can do a better job in helping students find what they are interested in. Just as a saying goes, interest is the best teacher, which clearly indicates the great role played by interest in all levels of education. The above claim is especially true of teens education, because they are in the puberty, the most rebellious period. Put it in another way, without the drive of their own interest, they are less likely to perform well in any activities required by their teachers. In this case, offering various activities to adolescents undoubtedly can more effectively stimulate their interest and passion. Once they find what they like, they will automatically put more efforts and time in it.

However, requiring all teenagers to solely engage in one activities may fail to cater to the interest of the majorities of them.

All in all, we can draw the conclusion that it is a wiser move for students to attend multiple activities, in order to obtain a holistic development and find what they are passionate for.

words

extracurricular activities 课外活动
there arises a controversy pertaining whether… or … 用来叙述矛盾
towards such a long-running tug-of-war
A can be conductive to B. A引起B
self-evident 不明自白的
To illustrate, … 用于说明原因
aesthetic 审美的
cultivate 培养, 熏陶
develop in an all-round way 全面发展
holistic development 全面发展
In contrast, … 用于反面描述
Put it in another way, … 用于反面描述
modern life necessitates the acquisition of multiple skills. 现代生活要求多样的技能
effectively stimulate their interest and passion 有效地激发兴趣和热情
puberty 青春期
rebellious period 叛逆期
adolescent 青少年
cater to the interest of the majorities of them 迎合大多数人的兴趣
it’s a wiser move for … to … 用于表述态度观点
passionate for 对…充满激情

interest is the best teacher.兴趣是最好的老师
, which clearly indicates … 用于阐述谚语

initially furthermore

有灵动词: equip necessitate

structure

开头: field+阐述问题+观点+原因如下
2-3个观点：
序数词+观点+举例子(比如题目里提到的例子)/谚语+阐述+反面论述+衔接下一个观点/总结一下
反面？
all in all

opinion

青少年是该参加多样的课外活动：

  1. 有助于全面发展
  2. 有助于探索自己的兴趣所在

Integrated writing

The Salinity of the Salton Sea has been increasing steadliy for years, and if the trend continues it will be too salty to support the fish and birds. The paper illustruted three methods to address this urgent problem, while the professor argued aginst all of the solutions.
The first solution is to to remove the salt from the water by vapor the

Independented writing

1. Essentially, a theroy is an abstract, symbolic representation of what is conceived to be reality.

  2. Thanks to modern irrigation, crops now grow abundantly in areas where once nothing but cacti and sagebrush could live.

  3. Anthorpologists have discovered that fear, happiness, sadness and surprise are universally reflected in facial expressions.

  4. Of the millions who saw Halley’s comet, how many people will live long enough to see it return in 21st century.

  5. The greater the population there is in a locality, the greater the need there is for water, transportation, and disposal of refuse.

  6. Perfectly matched pearls, strung into necklace, bring a far higher price than the same pearls sold individually.

  7. The development of mechanical timepieces spurred the search for more accurate sundials with which to regulate them.

  8. Studies of the gravity field of the Earth indicated that its crust and mantal yield when unisversal weight is placed on them.

  9. Quails typically have short rounded wings that enable them to spring into full flight instantly when disterbed in their hiding places.

  10. Fungi are important in the process of decay, which returns ingredients into the soil, enhances soil fertility, and decomposes animal debris.

  11. Most substance contract when they freeze so that the density of a substance’s soild is higher than the density of its liquid.

  12. By the middle of the twentieth centry, painters and sculptors in the United States had begun to exert a great worldwide influence over art.

  13. Although apparently rigid, bones exhibit a degree of elasticity that enables the skeleton to withstand considerable impact.

  14. Finally, the coughing reflex in reaction to irritants in the airway produces not a cough during sleep, but a cessation of breathing.

  15. But when consumers do not kown they are being labbied, they may accept claims they would otherwise suspicious of.

  16. Buyers and sellers should be aware of new developments in technology which can and does affect marketing activitis.

  17. With modern offices becoming more mechanized, designers are attempting to personlize them with warmer and less severe interiors.

  18. The worker and soldier castes of termites consists of both males and females, and the queen lives permanently with a male consort.

  19. Childern with parents whose gaidence are firm, consistent, and rational are inclined to possess high levels of self-confidence.

  20. Lucretia Mott’s influence was so significant that she has been credited by some authorities as the originator of feminism in the United States.

  21. No social crusade aroused Elizabeth Williams’ enthusiasm more than the expansion of educational facilities for immigrants to the United States.

  22. Since wind-pollinated flowers have no need to attract insects or other animals, they have dispensed with bright petals, nectar, and scent.

  23. In the absence of a tradition of classic stone-carving and building, the desire to develop Roman ameneties would have been difficult to fulfill.

  24. Out in remote areas of the countryside, in rural locations, music was more traditional; the same songs were enjoyed by previeous generations.

  25. In the second half of the millennium, int the south around the city of Uruk, there was an enormous escalation in the area ocuppied by permanent settlements.

  26. By fossils, we mean traces of prehistoric animals such as bones, which become mineralized, or impressions of bones or organs that are left in stone.

  27. The application of electronic controls made possible by the microprocessor and computer storage have mutiplied the uses of the modern typewriter.

  28. Even though the precise qualities of hero in literary words may vary over time, the basic exemplary function of the hero seems to remain constant.

  29. When Henry Ford first sought financial backing for making cars, the very notion of farmers and clerks owning automobiles was considered ridiculous.

  30. Many people who wanted to become archaeologists ended up pursuing other careers and contributing to archaeological research only as unpaid amateurs.

  31. Even before the discover of germs, the belidfs that disease spread by “miasmas”(noxious forms of bed air) prompted attention to sewers and open garbage.

  32. Since the consumers consider the best fruit to be that which is the most attractive, the grower must provide products that satisfy the discerning eye.

  33. With on ocaeans to dissolve it, outgased carbon dioxide began to accumulate in the atmosphere, intensifying the greenhouse effect even more.

  34. Buildings contribute to human life when they provide shelter, enrich space, complement their site, suit climate, and are ecnomically feasible.

  35. Research into the dynamics of storms is directed toward improving the ability to predict these event and thus to minimize the damage and avoid loss of life.

  36. Their streamlined bodies, the absence of hind legs, and the presence of a fluke and blowhole cannot disguise their affineties with land dwelling manmals.

  37. Scientists do not kown why dinosaurs became extinct, but some theories postulate that chages in geography, climate, and sea levels were responsible.

  38. The elimination of inflation would ensure that the amount of money used in repaying the loan would have the same value as the amount of money borrowed.

  39. Futurism, an early twentieth-century movement in art, rejected all traditions and attempted to glorify the contemporary life by emphasizing the machine and motion.

  40. Acids are chemical compounds that, in water solution , have a sharp taste, a corrosive action on metals, and the ability to turn certein blue vegetable dyes red.

  41. In other instances, such as the Induss River Valley, the cities lacked a royal presinct and the ostentatious palaces that marked their contemporaries elsewhere.

  42. Offshore drilling platforms extend the search for oil to the ocean's continental shevels–those gently sloping submarine regines at the edges of the continents.

  43. Even those artists who were most dependent on photography became reluctant to admit that they made use of it, in case this compromised their professinal standing.

  44. According to anthropologists, the earlist ancestors fo humans that stood upright resembled chimpanzees facially, with sloping forehead and protruding brows.

  45. Anarchism is a term discribing a cluster of dorctrines and attribudes whoes principal uniting feature is the belief that government is both harmful and unnecessary.

  46. A rotary engine attached to the steam engine enable shafts to be turned and machines to be driven, resulting in mills using steam power to spin and weave cotton.

  47. Self-awareness, even in its earlist stages, might entail an wareness of others, the ability to see their perspective and to look at the world from another’s point of view.

  48. Various committees acted as an executive branch, implementing policies of the assembly and supervising, for instance, the food and water supplies and public buildings.

  49. A mountain range may emerge and gradually split a population of organisms that can inhabit only lowland lakes; certain fash populations might become isolated in this way.

  50. Think of this way, humans originally needed to have a stronger sensitivity to bitter-tasting foods so they could learn what plants were good for them and which ones might be poisonous.

  51. Because eastern plows could not pernetrate the densely tangled roots of prairie grass, the earlist settlers erected farms along the boudary sparating the forest from the prairie.

  52. If the artificial Sun remained stationary, the birds would shift their direction with respect to it at a rate of about 15 degrees per hour, the Sun’s rate of movement across the sky.

  53. The Industrial Revolution had several roots, one of which was a commercial revolution that, beganning as far back as the sixteen century, accompanied Europe’s expansion overseas.

  54. The jaw joints of many advanced herbivores, such as cows, lie at a different level than the tooth row, allowing transverse tearing, shredding, and compression of plant material.

  55. So musical instruments evolved in ways that optimize their acoustical properties–how the instrument viberates and sends those viberation through the air to our eardrums.

  56. German printers had the disadvantage of working with complex typeface that the Italians sneeringly referred to as “Gothic” and that later became known as black letter.

  57. Elizabeth Blackwell, the first woman medical doctoe in the United States, founded the New York Infirmary, an institution that always had a compeletly female medical staff.

  58. We’re gone over where coral reefs are usually formed–along the edges of shallow ocean banks in the tropical or subtropical regions, and the fact that they are declining at an alarming rate.

  59. Raised beaches often consist of areas of sand, pebbles, or dunes, sometimes containing seashells or piles of debris comprising shells and bones of marine animals used by humans.

  60. But as more and moree accumulations of strata were cataloged in more and more places, it became clear that the sequences of rocks sometimes differed from region to region and that no rick type was ever going to be a reliable time marker throughout the world.

  61. This was an important innovation because the camel's ability to thrive in harsh desert conditions and to carry large loads cheaply made it an effective and efficient means of transportation.

  62. These people exploited the landscape intensively, foraging on hill slopes fo wild cereal grasses and nuts, while hunting gazelle and other game on grasssy lowland and in river valleys.

  63. The recognition that many aspects of modern flora and fauna were present on both side of the Bering Sea as remnents of the ice-age landscape led to this region being named Beringia.

  64. Thus, the recalling of an event (a hunt, battle, or other feat) is elaborated thought teh narrator's pantomime and impersonation and eventually through each role being assumed by a different person.

  65. The extreme seriousness of desertifiction results from the vast areas of land and the tremendous numbers of people affected, as well as from the grat diffculty of reversing or even slowing the process.

  66. Before starting on a sea voyage, prudent navigators learn the sea charts, study the sailing directions, and memorize lighthouse locations to prepare themselves for any conditions they might encounter.

  67. The question of why infantile amnesia occurs has intrigued phycologists for decades, especially in light of ample evidence that infants and young children can display impressive memory capabilities.

  68. Researchers can drill core samples, uh, basically get a cross-section of a tree without to kill it, look at the rings and get a picture of what the climate was like in the basin for each of the tree’s years.

  69. Greate britain’s better-developed banking and credit system also helped speed the industrial progresss, as did the fact that it was the home of an impressive array of entrepreneurs and inventors.

  70. Confronted with a multitude of unreliable figures, economists have compared the population records with the aggregate data for cultivated land area and grain production in the six century since 1368.

  71. Apparently, species-rich plots were likely to contain some drought-resistant plant species that grew better in drought years, compensating for the poor growth of less-tolerant species.

  72. Even more shocking is the fact that the number and rate of imprisonment have more than doubled over the past twenty years, and recidivism–that is the rate re-arrest–is more than 60 percent.

  73. These plants propagate by producing spore–tiny fertilized cells that contain all the instructions for making a new plant–but the spores are unprotected by any outer coating and carry no supply of nutrient.

  74. Meanwhile, the deadlist strains of the virus perished with their hosts as natural selection favored strains that could infect hosts nbut kill them. Thus, natural selection stabilized this host-parasite relationship.

  75. The residence time is the average length of time that any particular molecule of water remains in the lake, and it is calculated by dividing the voleme of water in the lake by the rate at which water leaves the lake.

  76. An eye is a collection of cells that are specialized for light detection through the prensence of phototsensitive pigment as well as the restricting the direction of incoming light that will strike the photosensitive cells.

  77. Sociologists view primary groups as bridges between individuals and the large society because they transmit, mediate, and interpret a society’s cultural patterns and provide the sense of oneness so critical for social salidarity.

  78. Importing the grain, which would have been expensive and timw consuming for Dutch to have produced themselves, kept the price low and thus stimulated individual demand for other foodstuffs and consumer goods.

  79. While the man who make faithful copies of sand paintings from the past represent the principle of steability inNavajo thought, women embody dynamism and create new designs for every weaving they make.

  80. In turn, a deep attachment to the land, and to the stability which rural life engenders, fostered the Roman virtue: gravitas, a sense of responsibility, pietas, a sense of devotion to family and country, and iustitia, a sense of natural order.

  81. In constract to mammals and birds, amphibians are unable to produce thermal energy though their metabolic activity, which wiould allow them to regulate their body temperature independent of the surrounding or ambient temperature.

  82. This is because the gaps among the original grains are often not totally plugged with cementing chemicals; also, parts of the original grains may dissolved by percolating groudwater, either while consolidation is taking place or at any time afterwards.

  83. The growth of mutual trust among merchants fasilitated the growth of sales on credits and led to new developments in finace, such as the bill of exchange, a device that made the long, slow, dengerous shipment of coins unnecessary.

  84. Widely reported, if somewhat distrusted, accounts by figures like the famous traveler from Venice, Marco Polo, of the willingness of people in China to trade with Europeans and of the immensity of the wealth to be gained by such contract made the idea irresistible.

  85. In the wake of the Roman Empire’s conquest of Britain in the first century A.D., a large troops stay in the new province, and these troops had a considerable impact on Britain with their camps, fortifications, and partcipation in local economy.

  86. While m,any programs designed for preschoolers focus primarily on social and emotional factors, some are geared mainly toward promoting cognitive gains and preparing preschoolers fir the formal instruction they will experience when they start kindergarten.

  87. Hero compiled descriptions and sketches of 70 some clever little, mechanical devices, most of which utilized compressed air to cause water, or insome case, wine to flow oneplace to another, or sometimes to squirt or to make some kind of noise.

  88. Introducing of genetically modified trees, designed for fast growth and high yield in given geographic conditions, would satisify the demand of wood in many o those areas save teh endengered native frees, which often enclude unique orr rare species.

  89. In the 1760s, James Watt perfected a separate condenser for the steam, so that the cylinder did not have to be cooled at every stroke; then he devised a way to make the piston turn a wheel and thus convert reciprocating (back and forth) motion into rotary motion.

  90. Unlike short-term financial coorperation between investors for a single commercial undertaking, joint-stock companies provided permanent funding of capital by drawing on the investors of merchants and other investors who purchased shares in the company.

  91. This was justified by the view that reflective practice could teachers to feel more intellectually involved in their role and work in teaching and enable them to cope with the paucity of scientific fact and the uncertanty of knowlegye in the discipline of teaching.

  92. There were no other public buildings, suggesting that the priests who were in charegded of temples also were responsible in governing the city and orgnizing people working in the fields and on irrigation projects building and maintaining systems of ditches and dams.

  93. Inequalities of wealth and rank certainliy exist, and have probably existed in most pastoralist societies, but except in periods of military conquest, they are normally too slight to generate the stable, hereditary hierarchies that are usually implied by the term class.

  94. Many ecologists now think that the relative long-term stability of climax communities comes from diversity but from the “patchness” of the environment; an environment that varies from palce to place supports more organisms than an environment that is uniform.

  95. According to conventional theory, yawning takes place when people are bored or sleepy and serves the function of increasing alertness by reversing, thought deeper breathing, the drop in blood oxygen levels that are caused by the shallow breathing that accompanies lack of sleep or boredom.

Well, just as the marathon was one of the concluding events of the Olympic Games, we’re bringing our broadcast marathon to a close beyond the competitive drama. Every Olympics provides a snapshot of a city and a country at a point of time. This one was more compelling than most since China’s rise and its onging transformation is the global story not only the moment, but likely the foreseeable future these Olympics were a milestone in that still unfolding story and well history will tell us whether or not the Olympics provided China with the confidence to not only build on its considerable strengths but also to address its considerable problems. This much we know, this is a country so vast, a people with lives so varied in a history so rich and complex that no visitor can fully grasp it. Still at these games no advanced degree in international relations was required to appreciate the genuine warmth of the Chinese people, the history pride in their country and how seriously Chinses citizens famous Olympics-everyday men and women took this chance to show themselves to the world. All Olympics are important to their city. These games were monumentally important to the host nation, which happens to be home to one-fifth of humanity. All that said just as these Olympics were significant politically, they were also very significant competitively and their Beijing turned out to be among the most memorable Olympics ever. One headline was antipated before the games began and then verified here for the first time since the fall of the Berlin Wall and ongoing Olympic rivalry shapes up in Beijing. The u.s. won 110 medals, the most it’s ever won at a non-american Olympics. But China second in overall count easily won the most goals here and the most by any country since the old Soviet Union in 1988. Many of China’s triuumphs went beyond excellence. Their perfection and precision simply beatiful to behold. China is now a sports power with a sophisticated state-surppoted sports system. He’ll be at or near the top the medal list in London and beyond. Meanwhile the Americans had plenty to cheer about beach volleyball pairs both prevailing Natia Liukin and Shawn Johnson winning gold and winning hearts, the men’s basketball team doing exactly what they set out to do, win yes, but also redeem and redefine the image and purpose of the u.s. program. These games reached multiple cresecendos. From the begining, there was Michael Phelps who now ranks among the very greatest Olympics ever and it was also now in the top tier of the best and most popular contemporay athletes in any sport unheard of for swimmer until Phelps. Just about the time Phelps left the pool, a Jamaican jet zoomed over the track. The Sprint’s are supposed to be decided by blinks of an eye, not by bolts of spead so astonishing that like the spectators, the competitors can only marvel at the worlds and history’s fastest man more than nations 204 participated here than any games before and more won medals 87 than ever before as well. But beyond the medal podium the Olympics remand a human panorama with many also-ran finishes and first round eliminations nonetheless represnting epic personal stories fully appreciated only by the participants themselves and families, friends and countrymen. These games began with Johnny Mo’s stunning opening ceremony so boldly conceived and brilliantly executed that it set a standard for such occasions unlikely to be equalled and tonight with more theatrical touches the curtain came dowm. So the people of world came to Beijing and the people of China extened their hand. You don’t have to speak a word of Mandarin to understand that i’ve been fortunate to be involved with many Olympics. And in many way, this has been the most memorable in no small part due to the efforts of a samll army of people who work tirelesslty to bring these games home to you. For these colleagues, I will always have enormous professional regard and personal gratitude the names of these talented men and women accompany this fianal montage of images of China and Olympic moments, moments we hope resonate with as they have with us. Goodnight, this one lat time from China.
The executive producer of the Olympic Games and the opening ceremony was Dick Ebersol. The producer of the Olympic Games and teh opening ceremony was David Neil. The director of the Olympic Games and opening ceremony it was Bucky guns.The president of NBC Olympic is Gray Zheng Kel.

entry.title: 0.4 有效数字缺失
entry.content:
误差

误差来源

  1. 模型误差
  2. 观测误差
  3. 方法误差
  4. 计算(舍入)误差

误差的基本计算

  1. 加法: e(x±y)=e(x)±e(y)e(x\pm y)=e(x)\pm e(y)e(x±y)=e(x)±e(y)
  2. 乘法: e(xy)=ye(x)+x∗e(y)e(xy)=ye(x)+x^*e(y)e(xy)=ye(x)+x∗e(y)
  3. 除法: e(xy)=ye(x)−xe(y)yy∗e(\frac{x}{y})=\frac{ye(x)-xe(y)}{yy^*}e(yx​)=yy∗ye(x)−xe(y)​

有效数字

当 xxx 的绝对误差限为某一位的半个单位, 则这一位到第一个非零位的位数称为 xxx 的有效位数.

小结

  1. 由误差的基本计算: 相近不做差, 小数不做除

entry.title: 0.3 实数的浮点表示
entry.content:
IEEE745 浮点数

定义

分类

例子


注：

  1. 第一位是符号位, 代表浮点数的正负.
  2. 第二位是指数位的第一位, 代表指数的正负:
    1. 当其为1时, 指数p>0p>0p>0
    2. 当其为0, 且其他符号位全为1时, p=0p=0p=0
    3. 当其为0, 且其他符号位不全为1时, p<0p<0p<0

特殊指数

(00000000000)2(00000000000)_2(00000000000)2​ 表示 2−10222^{-1022}2−1022
(11111111111)2(11111111111)_2(11111111111)2​ 表示 2∞2^\infty2∞ (尾数位必须全零)

机器精度

机器精度 εmach\varepsilon_{mach}εmach​ 是 111 和比 111 大的最小浮点数之间的距离.
nnn 位尾数的 IEEE754 浮点数 εmarch=2−n\varepsilon_{march}=2^{-n}εmarch​=2−n

εmach =2−52≈2.22×10−16\varepsilon_{\text {mach }}=2^{-52} \approx 2.22 \times 10^{-16}εmach ​=2−52≈2.22×10−16

舍入

最近原则

  1. 第53位尾数为0, 则在第52位之后舍去 (舍去)
  2. 第53位尾数为1, 第53位之后非全零, 则在第52位加一 (进位)
  3. 第53位尾数为1, 第53位之后全零, 则依据使第52位等于0选择舍去或进位:
    1. 第52位为1, 则进位
    2. 第52位为0, 则舍去

加法

对齐小数位 →\rightarrow→ 高精度加法寄存器 →\rightarrow→ 舍入回相应精度

数值计算的基本原则

重要指标

  1. 速度
  2. 精度

原则

尽量减少工作量 (乘除法次数)

如: 多项式计算使用秦九昭算法

保持数据稳定性

迭代算法要求在迭代的过程中误差不断减小
若迭代过程中误差不断增大, 可以考虑逆向迭代

小结

  1. 熟悉 IEEE754 浮点数的表示方法
  2. 熟悉数值计算的基本原则
  3. 相近不做差, 小数不做除

entry.title: 0.2 二进制数字
entry.content:
十进制转二进制

（...b1×22+b2×21+b0+b−1×2−1+b−2×2−2...）10=(...b2b1b0b−1b−2)2（...b_1\times2^{2}+ b_2\times 2^1+b_0+b_{-1}\times 2^{-1}+b_{-2}\times2^{-2}...）_{10}=(...b_2b_1b_0b_{-1}b_{-2})_2（...b1​×22+b2​×21+b0​+b−1​×2−1+b−2​×2−2...）10​=(...b2​b1​b0​b−1​b−2​)2​
从手工计算上来说：
小数点前: 略
小数点后: 每次乘2, 小于1则该位为0, 大于1则-1且该位为1

一些数字

27=128,28=256,29=512,210=1024,211=20482^7=128, 2^8=256, 2^9=512, 2^{10}=1024, 2^{11}=204827=128,28=256,29=512,210=1024,211=2048

entry.title: 1.1 随机事件与随机变量
entry.content:
随机现象

  1. 事前不可预言
  2. 大量重复实验呈现规律

随机实验

  1. 可在相同条件 (人为可控, 相对重要的条件) 下重复进行. (可重复性)
  2. 事情可明确实验的全部可能结果. (结果明确性)
  3. 实验前不能预言将出现哪一个. (不可预言性)

随机事件 (事件)

  1. 基于一定的实验目的
  2. 可能发生也可能不发生

基本事件

  1. 一次实验中必发生一个且仅发生一个
  2. 最简单 (不能分解)

复合事件

由若干基本事件组合而成的事件

必然事件 (Ω\OmegaΩ)

做一次随机实验必定发生的事件

不可能事件 (Φ\PhiΦ)

做一次随机实验必定不发生的事件

样本空间

定义

基本事件的对应元素全体所组成的集合 Ω\OmegaΩ 称为实验的样本空间
样本空间的元素称为样本点
注: 样本空间与实验目的有关!

各种事件在样本空间中的集合表示

事件集合
随机事件集合 Ω\OmegaΩ
基本事件单点集 {ω}\{\omega\}{ω}
复合事件子集 A⊂ΩA\subset\OmegaA⊂Ω
必然事件全集 Ω\OmegaΩ
不可能事件空集 Φ\PhiΦ

随机事件的运算

在一次实验中, 若实验结果 ω\omegaω 是 A 中元素, 即 ω∈A\omega\in Aω∈A , 则称事件 A 发生.

包含关系

A⊂BA\subset BA⊂B : 事件A发生, 则事件B必发生.

和事件

A∪BA\cup BA∪B : 事件A与事件B至少发生一个.

积事件

A∩BA\cap BA∩B (ABABAB): 事件A与事件B同时发生

互不相容

AB=∅AB=\emptysetAB=∅ : 事件A与事件B不能同时发生.

逆事件 (对立事件)

B=\hlineA (AB=∅∧A∪B=ΩAB=\emptyset\land A\cup B=\OmegaAB=∅∧A∪B=Ω)：事件A与事件B必发生一个且仅发生一个.

差事件

A−BA-BA−B : 事件A发生且事件B不发生.

小结

  1. 事件与样本空间都与"实验目的"有关

2022-09-10的日记

星期六
[[36_2022-09-09 | <<前一天的日记]] | [[36_2022-09-11 | 后一天的日记>>]]

重要的任务

我的微习惯

  • [x] 听听力 #微习惯/听力:6
  • [x] 一篇TOEFL阅读 #微习惯/TOEFL阅读:3
  • [x] 俯卧撑25个 #微习惯/俯卧撑:25
  • [x] 开合跳100个 #微习惯/开合跳:100
  • [x] 下蹲20个 #微习惯/下蹲:20

急需处理的事

学习目标

  • [ ] 数学改错 #task
  • [ ] 给学术领航导生发邮件 #task

一些随想

#一些随想 虽然你之前的TOEFL阅读与听力，在某些时候有较好的发挥，但今天的练习证明你的英语水平依旧十分低下，距离你的TOEFL目标分数依旧十分遥远，之前练习时的好运并不会每次都眷顾你(2023.1.8)，唯有忍辱负重，砥砺前行，每天坚持进行英语的输入输出，将你真实的英语水平提高上去，你才能得到你想要的分数。
此外，这两天都没有跑步，你的精神状态也一天不如一天，所以你每天早晨要坚持跑步。你还要考虑需不需要将跑步列入你的微习惯之中。

本周未完成的任务

2022-09-03的日记

星期六
[[35_2022-09-02 | <<前一天的日记]] | [[35_2022-09-04 | 后一天的日记>>]]

重要的任务

我的微习惯

  • [ ] 听听力 #微习惯/听力:
  • [x] 一篇TOEFL阅读 #微习惯/TOEFL阅读:1
  • [x] 俯卧撑25个 #微习惯/俯卧撑:25
  • [x] 开合跳100个 #微习惯/开合跳:100
  • [x] 下蹲20个 #微习惯/下蹲:20

急需处理的事

学习目标

一些随想

#一些随想 明天上午有网课平台测试，后天就正式开学，开始上课了。暑假结束了，想想时间过得真快啊 。6月21日暑假开始到今天9月3日，在学校一共7+21=28天，在家47天，一共75天，即两个半月。
先说在家的时间，基本上就只有躺床上沙发上和在桌前打游戏看视频两个状态，一整天下来，人是打不起精神的，没有足够的学习和运动的动力，懒惰散漫成为常态，人长胖了，变得怠惰拖沓，成天空想而很少付诸行动 ，即使踏出一步两步，那仅有的热情也会因为没有足够的毅力支撑，很快就会被自己的懒惰磨灭掉。
再说在学校的时间，在学校人少，特别是在宿舍只有我一人时，内心会平静很多，会有更多的时间思考，但空想主义似乎仍会占据我的大多数时间，行动力与毅力仍会不足，而且学习生活的效率也会十分低下。在人多的时候，宿舍还有其他人的时候，我会变得浮躁，很难沉下心来思考，于是一开始我的动力会增加，学习生活的效率会提高，但久而久之对自我的控制力依旧会下降，会出现三天打鱼两天晒网的情况。
在学校时，我会渴望在家中的宁静与时间上的自由；在家时，我会渴望在学校时的动力、没有家长的打扰、行为上的自由。于是我就陷入了在两者之间反复横跳，无法同时拥有沉浸思考之心与高效行动能力的恶性循环之中。

如何跳出这个循环？林瑞康，你现在只能先从思想觉悟上“跳出”这个怪圈，但从客观事实上，你会仍然并且长久地身处于这个怪圈之中，在将来的很长一段时间，你会有强烈的无助感（就像踩在棉花上无从着力），所以你需要一份计划来帮助你找到“使劲”的方向。这份计划就像“一条绳子”：这份计划需要有一个宏大的终极目标以确保它能持续到你走出怪圈并且看清世界找到下一个人生目标的时候；它必须要由一个一个切实可行的小目标构成以确保它足够结实不会在你发力的时候断掉；这绳子可能会随着时间的推移而发生变化，但就像革命一样：“革命就像推锯子，有时向前，有时向后，但总的来说是向深处发展的”，你的计划再怎么变化也应该是向着你的最终的目标发展的。
有了这份计划，林瑞康，你剩下需要做的就是紧紧地抓住这条绳子，使劲地向上爬。你会在中途感到满足感，但这份满足感这是暂时的，甚至是对计划的执行有害的，因为它极有可能来自于你的自负与懒惰。你会感到负罪感，想要停下来向下看，也许还会有人把你向下拽，但请你坚持抬头紧盯绳子与目标，不要在意他人的目光与感受，一切都会过去的。你会感到疲惫感，想要停下来，想要躺下来，这是因为你的目标还不够明确，使命感还不够强烈，你心中的那一团火燃烧得还不够纯净，还不够旺盛。你不正当的欲望也会变得强烈，但请你记住，你是在和时间比赛谁先到达终点，请你把这些欲望能立刻汇聚到动力之中的汇聚起来，否则立刻扔掉它们、切断与它们的联系，它们靠近你一次你就踢开他们一次，你需要明白你与它们的关系“你想要什么，你不想要什么，你可以放弃什么？”(2023.1.8)

新学期的计划：[[大二上的学期计划]]

这是这学期的开学礼物：魔杖店的慈父之声: 奥利凡德读诗"IF",留给世间的励志声音_哔哩哔哩_bilibili

本周未完成的任务
